items.briefing:
Australia's cybersecurity framework is set for a significant change, with the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) announcing it will retire the Essential Eight security guidelines within the next two years. Organisations currently using the Essential Eight as their compliance benchmark should watch for updates on what will replace it and begin thinking about how to adapt. Meanwhile, a new app called RealCheck from Bitdefender has launched to help people identify AI-generated deepfake images and videos — a growing concern for staff who receive unsolicited media or verify identities online.

**Practical tip:** Check whether your organisation's security policies reference the Essential Eight, and flag it with your IT or compliance team so you're ready when the replacement framework is announced.

featured.title: ASD to retire Essential Eight within two years, consults on replacement
featured.quote: ASD retiring Essential Eight cybersecurity framework requires Australian organisations to prepare for major compliance changes.
